title: 2025 08 16 That Girl Type App
html:
TL;DR

  • Mid-August 2025 brainstorm document spanning 5+ adjacent app concepts — anonymous gossip, AI-generated outcomes, daily ritual mechanics — that ultimately converge on irl lore as the wholesome pivot.
  • Concepts proposed: Void Radar (Yik-Yak-style daily prompts with valid/delulu/petty tags), AI Tea Oracle (one-line vent → AI prophecy with odds), Red Flag Forensics (paste behavior → AI severity report), Tea Spill Hour (BeReal-style daily 1hr window), and IRL Lore itself.
  • All variants share a "you must spill to unlock" mechanic — solving the BeReal-style cold-start by gating consumption on contribution.
  • "Anonymity is going to be very crucial here" + "long onboarding flow explaining vision is important" are the two hard constraints Yahir wrote at the top.
  • Doc shows the brainstorm-to-product-spec arc: 5 concepts collapse into IRL Lore by the end, which then gets a full PRD in the two weeks later doc.

Notes

The "Tea Oracle" variant is the AI-twist version: each post gets passed through an LLM that reimagines it as a dramatic prophecy or "flag report" with odds and warnings. The most-rated outputs become Prophecy Cards — viral artifacts. This aesthetic ("cultural currency of the app", "fandom release") connects to AI as product surface thinking — using LLMs not just as backend but as the felt brand layer.

The pivot moment is captured verbatim in the doc: "I want to update this to make it more wholesome where it's more for personal use... a vault to see what been happening with your life. The Spotify wrap happens with your life at the end of the week, month, years." This is the line that births IRL Lore as Yahir's public-facing app.

The "modified idea" trailing block describes the timeline-as-tributary visual metaphor — zoom in on month → week → day, with prediction cards nested at each layer. Beautiful to read; would be hard to build.

Connections

  • irl lore — the pivot endpoint that gets a full PRD next
  • — sibling project basket
  • dream app — earlier ambient-AI app concept in the same lineage
  • — the personal-archive problem these apps target
